feedback.text: My wish is, to have a legislation for computer driven service hotlines for companies. Nearly all of the companies, where I am a client at the moment have a quiet annoying telephone service hotline. Before I get an human operator, to whom I can talk to, a combination of at least 2 of these hurdles must be passed: - An artificial intelligence to whom you should say a keyword what your concern is. The computer does not understand you very well - Some commercial advertisement about some of the companies products - A telephone computer where you go through a menu by dialing numbers on your telephones keypad - Some information where to get service on a webpage or FAQ - An annoying, repetive music for an average time of 25 minutes - which is mostly paused for some jingles or slogans of the company Sometimes after staying in the line for extensive period of time, a disconnection (hung up the phone) is initiated by the company. My suggestion to solve these problems would be, to give a legislation, so that EU citiziens do not waste their time. Just some ideas: - Getting to the final waiting queue may at most take 1 minute - The waiting queue should be silent except for a call of estimated waiting time in minutes, every minute - If the computer recognizes your phone number, it is offered, that you will be called back, when an operator is available or the call center is less busy
